title: Spinal Cord Stimulation for the Failed Back Syndrome
study_question: Is spinal cord stimulation effective for treating failed back syndrome pain?
population_assessed: 32 patients with failed back syndrome receiving trial of dorsal spinal cord stimulation.
outcome_measures: Pain improvement, employment status, narcotic use, complications
inclusion_criteria: Failed back syndrome patients with lumbar pain, prior laminectomy, no mechanical instability.
answer_to_study_question: SCS provided good pain relief in 87% at 1 month, declining to 37.5% at 5 years. Narcotic use decreased significantly.
